title: Drone Delivery Swarm: Mission-Validity Rule Induction
isolation: candidate runs as an isolated stdin->stdout subprocess via isorun; query labels, the hidden capacity D, and oracle state stay in the evaluator process
strategies: majority TRAIN label for every query (reproduces the evaluator baseline -> ~0.1; no rule, no generalization)
well-nesting (Dyck-K) classifier only: recovers the type-matched LIFO structure but ignores the hidden airspace capacity -> wrongly accepts over-capacity missions

capacity-aware inducer: Dyck-K well-nesting PLUS an inferred max-airborne-depth threshold; length-independent so it generalizes to the long OOD queries, but conservative at the unseen boundary -> strong, not perfect

richer inducers (learn the depth threshold with a margin/extrapolation, statistical n-gram or small learned classifiers, or full grammar+constraint induction) that push accuracy higher on both ID and length-OOD splits
